subject: Public Health
title: Misinformation During Epidemics
description: A short lesson to help you apply Misinformation During Epidemics.

hook.text: During the Black Death, people blamed Jews. During COVID-19, people blamed 5G towers.

buildup.text: Fear and uncertainty create fertile ground for conspiracy theories and scapegoating.

discovery.text: The WHO coined 'infodemic'—an excess of information making it hard to find trustworthy guidance.

twist.text: Social media spreads misinformation faster than any virus—a lie travels six times faster than the truth.

climax.text: Countering misinformation requires prebunking—teaching critical thinking before the crisis hits.

punchline.text: Bad information is its own kind of epidemic.

quiz.question: What is an 'infodemic'?
options: An excess of information making trustworthy guidance hard to find
A digital virus that infects computers
